name: there-are-rivers-in-the-sky
description: Elif Shafak's There Are Rivers in the Sky — a novel flowing across centuries and continents, using a single drop of water to connect ancient Nineveh to modern London through storytelling, memory, and the fragility of civilization.
useCase: Finding meaning through storytelling, grieving and rebuilding, understanding connection across time, facing mortality, finding empathy across difference, exploring identity between worlds.
knownRisks: This is a literary novel — themes are interpretive
The non-linear structure may not suit readers seeking direct frameworks
The novel deals with trauma and loss — handle with sensitivity
